title: SSVEP fatigue dataset with two frequency paradigms
description: A steady-state visually evoked potential (SSVEP) electroencephalography dataset designed to investigate the effects of mental fatigue on brain-computer interface performance. The dataset comprises 24 healthy participants performing gaze-shifting tasks across 32 visual stimulation frequencies (8-15 Hz and 25.5-33 Hz) in two sessions, with explicit fatigue manipulation through dynamic stopping strategies. High-resolution 64-channel EEG recordings sampled at 1000 Hz enable investigation of frequency-dependent neural responses and fatigue-related performance degradation in SSVEP-based BCIs.
methods_description: EEG data were acquired from 24 healthy participants (12 male, 12 female, ages 18-26) using a 64-channel Synamps2 system (Neuroscan) with standard 1005 montage referenced to Cz. Sampling rate was 1000 Hz with online bandpass filtering (0.15-200 Hz) and 50 Hz line frequency notch filtering. Impedance was maintained below 10 kOhm. Participants performed gaze-shifting tasks with 32 visual stimulation frequencies across two sessions, with 60 blocks per session (6 training + 24 fatigue blocks per frequency condition). Trial duration was 2 seconds with inter-trial intervals of 0.14-2.14 seconds.
